product.name: insightmesh-core
product.one_liner: A cognitive knowledge engine that learns over time: multi-agent reasoning + evolving memory + dynamic wiki views.
problem: Chat threads and document searches are fragmented, making it hard to retain insights across sessions.
Traditional retrieval systems treat knowledge as static and forget past understanding.
Users and teams need systems that can remember, reason, and evolve contextually over time.
value: Persistent and evolving memory that reflects real-time understanding, not just static facts.
Structured, traceable answers built from diverse inputs, not just a single reply.
A knowledge base that grows with the user — offering context, history, and collaboration.

personas.success: fewer duplicate searches, faster synthesis, clearer citations
faster onboarding, smoother handoff, reduced rework
cleaner communication, visible change tracking, proactive insights

must_have: Support for orchestrated reasoning across multiple agents or perspectives.
Ability to retrieve relevant prior knowledge alongside new information.
Tools to evolve and version knowledge over time, with traceable source attribution.
Dynamic, user-friendly outputs (e.g. wiki-style pages) for reuse and collaboration.
Ability to capture, review, and accept edits to the knowledge base incrementally.
nice_to_have: Ability to detect conflicting or outdated knowledge and flag for resolution.
Personalized topic watchlists or summaries that reflect changes over time.
Integration adapters for external systems (docs, repos, communication tools).
out_of_scope_initially: Advanced billing or multi-tenant subscription models.
Custom model hosting or fine-tuning pipelines.

happy_path: User creates a workspace and adds reference materials (documents, notes, etc).
They ask a complex question, which triggers a multi-perspective reasoning process.
The system retrieves relevant information (from the LLM and/or a web search) and past context.
It generates an answer and a wiki-style page with citations and editable content.
The user reviews the result and can edit or approve it, locking in the learning.
Later questions build on this evolving knowledge instead of starting from scratch.
alternates: If no source material is available, guide the user to add context or fetch from allowed tools.
If conflicting information arises, mark it and suggest a reconciliation flow.

acceptance_criteria: The system can answer multi-step questions using a mix of current and prior knowledge.
Users can review, edit, and version knowledge entries over time.
Each answer includes transparent citations and an option to view prior revisions.
Knowledge growth is visible and traceable — follow-up queries improve as context deepens.
metrics: User-rated usefulness of answers (e.g. via Likert scale)
Growth rate of the knowledge base vs. edits or conflicts
Repeat query success rate using enriched memory

risks.mitigation: Start with simple structures and evolve based on real usage patterns

risks.risk: User trust drops if answers appear inconsistent or untraceable
risks.mitigation: Ensure all answers are backed by transparent citations and revision history
Use review workflows and drift detection to maintain clarity
